diagrams.title: Basic Computer System Organization
diagrams.description: Shows the fundamental components of a computer system including CPU, memory, I/O devices, and their interconnections
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how hardware components communicate and work together in a computer system

diagrams.title: Memory Hierarchy Pyramid
diagrams.description: Illustrates the levels of memory from fastest/smallest to slowest/largest
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing the trade-off between speed, size, and cost in memory design and why caching is essential

diagrams.title: Interrupt Processing Flow
diagrams.description: Shows the sequence of events when an interrupt occurs
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how interrupts enable efficient handling of asynchronous events

diagrams.title: DMA Data Transfer Operation
diagrams.description: Demonstrates how DMA controller manages data transfer between I/O device and memory
elements: CPU sets up DMA controller with source, destination, and transfer size
Data transferred directly between I/O and memory
DMA releases bus, sends completion interrupt
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how DMA improves system performance by offloading data transfer from CPU
